title: Write 'How to Add a New Distributed Simulation' contributor guide
description:
CONTRIBUTING.md has guides for adding system design nodes (line 35-46) and algorithms (line 49-60) but NOTHING for distributed simulations. A new contributor must reverse-engineer the pattern from existing code — at least 1 hour wasted.

Write a section in CONTRIBUTING.md covering:
1. Prerequisites: understand the module architecture (link to module README)
2. Files to create: engine in src/lib/distributed/, types in index.ts
3. Files to modify: DistributedSim type, SIMULATIONS array, canvas/controls/properties switch chains, module-progress.ts
4. Template: annotated copy of a simple simulation (saga.ts is 122 lines — good template)
5. Checklist: engine works, canvas renders, controls function, properties filled, tests pass, step descriptions explain WHY
6. Common mistakes: forgetting the switch chain entry (silent blank canvas), forgetting module-progress entry

This saves every future contributor 1-2 hours.
acceptanceCriteria: CONTRIBUTING.md has 'How to Add a New Distributed Simulation' section
Lists all files to create and modify with paths
Includes annotated template from a simple sim
Includes checklist (engine, canvas, controls, properties, tests, WHY steps)
Includes common mistakes section

title: Write module README with architecture overview and data flow diagram
description:
No README exists in src/lib/distributed/ or src/components/modules/distributed/. A new developer has no map of the module.

Create src/lib/distributed/README.md with:
1. Architecture diagram (Mermaid): DistributedWrapper → useDistributedModule hook → 11 engines → 11 canvases
2. Data flow: user action → handler → engine.step() → events → canvas re-render
3. File inventory: which file does what (engine files, god file, learn panel, orphaned viz)
4. State management: 45 useState vars in hook, engine refs, timer management
5. Known issues: god file needs splitting, orphaned visualizers exist, progress tracking incomplete

This is the 'map' that saves every developer their first 2 hours of exploration.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md exists in src/lib/distributed/
Contains Mermaid architecture diagram
Contains data flow explanation
Lists all files with their purpose
Notes known architectural issues

title: Consolidate test file locations (currently split across two directories)
description:
Tests are in TWO locations:
- src/__tests__/lib/distributed/ (6 files: raft, consistent-hash, gossip, cap-theorem, 2pc, paxos)
- src/lib/distributed/__tests__/ (1 file: distributed-systems.test.ts)

A developer looking for 'where are the Raft tests' would find one location and miss the other. Consolidate to ONE location. Recommend: src/lib/distributed/__tests__/ (co-located with source, standard pattern).
acceptanceCriteria: All distributed test files in one directory
No test files in the other directory
All tests still pass from pnpm test
Test file names match engine file names

title: Add exhaustive switch statements to replace if/else chains for simulation routing
acceptanceCriteria: All three routing chains (canvas, controls, properties) use exhaustive switch
Adding a new sim to DistributedSim creates compile errors at every switch
The `never` assertion in default catches missing cases
Existing behavior unchanged

title: Add helpful error messages for common developer mistakes
acceptanceCriteria: Dev-only console.error for missing SIMULATIONS entry
Dev-only console.error for missing canvas/controls/properties handler
Errors only in development (stripped in production)
Messages include the file path where the fix should be applied

title: Write content style guide for distributed simulation descriptions and step text
description:
Teaching quality audit (Audit 3) found all 11 concepts score F or D because content follows no style guide. Without rules, each new contributor writes different quality content.

Create docs/content-style-guide-distributed.md:
1. Descriptions: MUST start with a problem, not a definition. Template: '[Problem statement]. [Name] solves this by [mechanism].'
2. Step descriptions: MUST explain WHY, not just WHAT. Template: '[Action] because [reason].'
3. Properties panel: MUST include 'Used By' (3+ real systems), 'Key Concepts' (3-5 bullets), 'Common Mistakes' (2+ items), 'Summary' (3 bullet flashcard).
4. Examples of good vs bad content for each section.

This ensures every new simulation meets the teaching quality bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Content style guide exists with templates for each section
Good/bad examples for descriptions, steps, properties
Linked from CONTRIBUTING.md in the distributed simulation section
Templates are copy-pasteable (not just principles)

title: Add JSDoc to all public engine methods missing documentation
description:
25 JSDoc annotations exist across 11 engine files, but coverage is uneven. Classes and constructors have docs; individual public methods often don't.

Missing JSDoc (sample):
- ConsistentHashRing.getKeyRange() at consistent-hash.ts:206 — no @param, no @returns
- GossipProtocol.getConvergenceStatus() at gossip.ts:215 — no docs
- CAPCluster.hasDivergence() at cap-theorem.ts:342 — no docs
- VectorClockSimulation.areConcurrent() at vector-clock.ts:187 — no docs

Add JSDoc with @param, @returns, and brief description to all exported public methods. This enables IDE hover documentation for contributors.
acceptanceCriteria: All exported public methods have JSDoc with @param and @returns
IDE hover shows useful documentation for each method
No public method left without at least a one-line description

title: Create PR review checklist for distributed simulation contributions
description:
No quality checklist exists for PRs adding new distributed simulations. A reviewer must manually check everything found across 9 audits.

Create .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/distributed-simulation.md:
- [ ] Engine file in src/lib/distributed/ with class/function export
- [ ] Types exported from index.ts barrel
- [ ] Added to DistributedSim union type
- [ ] Added to SIMULATIONS array with name + description
- [ ] Canvas component renders correctly
- [ ] Controls panel with appropriate buttons
- [ ] Properties panel with Key Concepts, Used By, Summary
- [ ] Step descriptions explain WHY not just WHAT
- [ ] At least 6 test cases covering edge cases
- [ ] No `any` or `unknown` types without justification
- [ ] Colors from shared palette (no hardcoded hex)
- [ ] prefers-reduced-motion respected
- [ ] Added to module-progress.ts features list
acceptanceCriteria: PR template exists for distributed simulation PRs
Covers engine, UI, content, testing, type safety, accessibility
Reviewers can check boxes during review
Template linked from CONTRIBUTING.md

title: Add warning comment to orphaned visualizers explaining they are NOT active
acceptanceCriteria: Warning comment at top of all 3 orphaned files
Comment points to the actual active location
Comment references the integration task IDs

title: Create scaffold script for new distributed simulations
description:
Adding a simulation requires creating 1 file and modifying 3 others across 10 locations. A scaffold script would generate the boilerplate and update all registration points.

```bash
pnpm scaffold:distributed-sim --id consistent-broadcast --name 'Consistent Broadcast'

Creates:
  src/lib/distributed/consistent-broadcast.ts (template with TODOs)
  src/lib/distributed/__tests__/consistent-broadcast.test.ts (template)

Updates:
  src/lib/distributed/index.ts (adds barrel export)
  src/components/modules/DistributedModule.tsx:
    - DistributedSim union (adds 'consistent-broadcast')
    - SIMULATIONS array (adds entry)
    - switch chains (adds placeholder cases)
  src/lib/progress/module-progress.ts (adds to features)

Prints:
  ✓ Created consistent-broadcast. Next steps:
  1. Implement the engine in consistent-broadcast.ts
  2. Design the canvas visualization
  3. Fill in properties panel content
  4. Write tests
  5. Run pnpm typecheck && pnpm test
```

This saves 30-60 minutes per new simulation and eliminates forgotten registration steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Script creates engine and test template files
Script updates all registration points (type, array, switches, barrel, progress)
Generated code compiles and renders a placeholder canvas
Script prints clear next-steps instructions
